# Redmine - project management software
# Copyright (C) 2006-  Jean-Philippe Lang
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or

# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.

class Issue < ApplicationRecord
  include Redmine::SafeAttributes
  include Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation
  include Redmine::I18n

  before_validation :default_assign, on: :create
  before_validation :force_default_value_on_noneditable_custom_fields, on: :create
  before_validation :clear_disabled_fields
  before_save :set_parent_id
  include Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet
  include Redmine::Reaction::Reactable

  belongs_to :project
  belongs_to :tracker
  belongs_to :status, :class_name => 'IssueStatus'
  belongs_to :author, :class_name => 'User'
  belongs_to :assigned_to, :class_name => 'Principal'
  belongs_to :fixed_version, :class_name => 'Version'
  belongs_to :priority, :class_name => 'IssuePriority'
  belongs_to :category, :class_name => 'IssueCategory'

  has_many :journals, :as => :journalized, :dependent => :destroy, :inverse_of => :journalized
  has_many :time_entries, :dependent => :destroy
  has_and_belongs_to_many :changesets, lambda {order("#{Changeset.table_name}.committed_on ASC, #{Changeset.table_name}.id ASC")}

  has_many :relations_from, :class_name => 'IssueRelation', :foreign_key => 'issue_from_id', :dependent => :delete_all
  has_many :relations_to, :class_name => 'IssueRelation', :foreign_key => 'issue_to_id', :dependent => :delete_all

  acts_as_attachable :after_add => :attachment_added, :after_remove => :attachment_removed
  acts_as_customizable
  acts_as_watchable
  acts_as_searchable :columns => ['subject', "#{table_name}.description"],
                     :preload => [:project, :status, :tracker],
                     :scope => lambda {|options| options[:open_issues] ? self.open : self.all}

  acts_as_event :title => Proc.new {|o| "#{o.tracker.name} ##{o.id} (#{o.status}): #{o.subject}"},
                :url => Proc.new {|o| {:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => o.id}},
                :type => Proc.new {|o| 'issue' + (o.closed? ? '-closed' : '')}

  acts_as_activity_provider :scope => proc {preload(:project, :author, :tracker, :status)},
                            :author_key => :author_id

  acts_as_mentionable :attributes => ['description']
  acts_as_webhookable
  include Issue::Webhookable

  DONE_RATIO_OPTIONS = %w(issue_field issue_status)

  attr_reader :transition_warning
  attr_writer :deleted_attachment_ids
  delegate :notes, :notes=, :private_notes, :private_notes=, :to => :current_journal, :allow_nil => true

  validates_presence_of :subject, :project, :tracker
  validates_presence_of :priority, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.priority_id_changed?}
  validates_presence_of :status, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.status_id_changed?}
  validates_presence_of :author, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.author_id_changed?}

  validates_length_of :subject, :maximum => 255
  validates_inclusion_of :done_ratio, :in => 0..100
  validates :estimated_hours, :numericality => {:greater_than_or_equal_to => 0, :allow_nil => true, :message => :invalid}
  validates :start_date, :date => true
  validates :due_date, :date => true
  validate :validate_issue, :validate_required_fields, :validate_permissions

  scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
    joins(:project).
    where(Issue.visible_condition(args.shift || User.current, *args))
  end)

  scope :open, (lambda do |*args|
    is_closed = !args.empty? ? !args.first : false
    joins(:status).
    where(:issue_statuses => {:is_closed => is_closed})
  end)

  scope :recently_updated, lambda {order(:updated_on => :desc)}
  scope :on_active_project, (lambda do
    joins(:project).
    where(:projects => {:status => Project::STATUS_ACTIVE})
  end)
  scope :fixed_version, (lambda do |versions|
    ids = [versions].flatten.compact.map {|v| v.is_a?(Version) ? v.id : v}
    ids.any? ? where(:fixed_version_id => ids) : none
  end)
  scope :assigned_to, (lambda do |arg|
    arg = Array(arg).uniq
    ids = arg.map {|p| p.is_a?(Principal) ? p.id : p}
    ids += arg.grep(User).map(&:group_ids).flatten.uniq
    ids.compact!
    ids.any? ? where(:assigned_to_id => ids) : none
  end)
  scope :like, (lambda do |q|
    if q.present?
      where(*::Query.tokenized_like_conditions("#{table_name}.subject", q))

  def create_parent_issue_journal
    return if persisted? && !saved_change_to_parent_id?
    return if destroyed? && @without_nested_set_update

    child_id = self.id
    old_parent_id, new_parent_id =
      if persisted?
        [parent_id_before_last_save, parent_id]
      elsif destroyed?
        [parent_id, nil]
      else
        [nil, parent_id]
      end

    if old_parent_id.present?
      Issue.transaction do
        if old_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(old_parent_id)
          old_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
          old_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', child_id, nil)
          old_parent_issue.save
        end
      end
    end

    if new_parent_id.present?
      Issue.transaction do
        if new_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(new_parent_id)
          new_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
          new_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', nil, child_id)
          new_parent_issue.save
        end
      end
    end
  end

    if author&.active? &&
        author.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, project) &&

def find_issue
    # Issue.visible.find(...) can not be used to redirect user to the login form
    # if the issue actually exists but requires authentication
    @issue = Issue.find(params[:id])
    raise Unauthorized unless @issue.visible?

    @project = @issue.project
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    render_404
  end

  # Find issues with a single :id param or :ids array param
  # Raises a Unauthorized exception if one of the issues is not visible
  def find_issues
    @issues = Issue.
      where(:id => (params[:id] || params[:ids])).
      preload(:project, :status, :tracker, :priority,
              :author, :assigned_to, :relations_to,
              {:custom_values => :custom_field}).
      to_a
    raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound if @issues.empty?
    raise Unauthorized unless @issues.all?(&:visible?)

    @projects = @issues.filter_map(&:project).uniq
    @project = @projects.first if @projects.size == 1
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    render_404
  end

  def find_attachments
    if (attachments = params[:attachments]).present?
      att = attachments.values.collect do |attachment|
        Attachment.find_by_token(attachment[:token]) if attachment[:token].present?

def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
    Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues, options) do |role, user|
      sql =
        if user.id && user.logged?
          case role.issues_visibility
          when 'all'
            '1=1'
          when 'default'
            user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
            "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false} " \
              "OR #{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} " \
              "OR #{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
          when 'own'
            user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
            "(#{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} OR " \
              "#{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
          else
            '1=0'
          end
        else
          "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false})"
        end
      unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
        tracker_ids = role.permissions_tracker_ids(:view_issues)
        if tracker_ids.any?
          sql = "(#{sql} AND #{table_name}.tracker_id IN (#{tracker_ids.join(',')}))"
        else
          sql = '1=0'
        end
      end
      sql
    end
  end

  # Returns true if usr or current user is allowed to view the issue
  def visible?(usr=nil)
    (usr || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_issues, self.project) do |role, user|
      visible =
        if user.logged?
          case role.issues_visibility
          when 'all'
            true
          when 'default'
            !self.is_private? || (self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to))
          when 'own'

def notified_users
    # Author and assignee are always notified unless they have been
    # locked or don't want to be notified
    notified = [author, assigned_to, previous_assignee].compact.uniq
    notified = notified.map {|n| n.is_a?(Group) ? n.users : n}.flatten
    notified.uniq!
    notified = notified.select {|u| u.active? && u.notify_about?(self)}

    notified += project.notified_users
    notified += project.users.preload(:preference).select(&:notify_about_high_priority_issues?) if priority.high?
    notified.uniq!
    # Remove users that can not view the issue
    notified.reject! {|user| !visible?(user)}
    notified
  end

  # Returns the email addresses that should be notified
  def recipients
    notified_users.collect(&:mail)
  end

  def notify?
    @notify != false
  end

  def notify=(arg)
    @notify = arg
  end

I now have the complete picture across all seven steps. Here is the audit.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Redmine, a Rails project/issue tracker (`config/routes.rb` entry point; 1,113 Ruby files; Rails MVC layout).

**Layout:** `app/models` (domain), `app/controllers` (request handling), `app/helpers` + `app/views/*.erb` (rendering), `lib/redmine` (framework internals: Gantt, nested-set, i18n, query engine), `lib/plugins/acts_as_*` (the `acts_as` behavior mixins), `db/migrate` (schema), `test/unit` + `test/functional` (tests).

**Central record:** `Issue` — `app/models/issue.rb:20` (`class Issue < ApplicationRecord`, ~2,150 lines). It is the 3rd-ranked hub (154+ callers). Shared pieces it's built on: `Project`, `Tracker`, `IssueStatus`, `IssuePriority`, `IssueCategory`, `Version`, `User/Principal`, `Journal`, `TimeEntry`, `Attachment`, `IssueRelation`, `Changeset`, and the `IssueQuery` reporting engine.

## Step 2 — The Issue contract

**Model:** `app/models/issue.rb:20`.

**Plugin-style behaviors (acts_as):**
- `acts_as_attachable` — `issue.rb:48` → `lib/plugins/acts_as_attachable/lib/acts_as_attachable.rb:52` (attachments `as: :container, dependent: :destroy`)
- `acts_as_customizable` — `issue.rb:49` → `lib/plugins/acts_as_customizable/lib/acts_as_customizable.rb:28` (custom_values `dependent: :delete_all`, `before_destroy :store_attachment_custom_value_ids`)
- `acts_as_watchable` — `issue.rb:50` → `lib/plugins/acts_as_watchable/lib/acts_as_watchable.rb:29` (watchers `dependent: :delete_all`)
- `acts_as_searchable` — `issue.rb:51` → `lib/plugins/acts_as_searchable/lib/acts_as_searchable.rb:39`
- `acts_as_event` — `issue.rb:55` → `lib/plugins/acts_as_event/lib/acts_as_event.rb:28`
- `acts_as_activity_provider` — `issue.rb:59` → `lib/plugins/acts_as_activity_provider/lib/acts_as_activity_provider.rb:28`
- `acts_as_mentionable` — `issue.rb:62` → `lib/redmine/acts/mentionable.rb:28`
- `acts_as_webhookable` — `issue.rb:63` → `lib/redmine/acts/webhookable.rb:28`

**Included modules:** `Redmine::SafeAttributes` (`issue.rb:21`), `Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation` (`:22`), `Redmine::I18n` (`:23`), `Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet` (`:29`, parent/child tree), `Redmine::Reaction::Reactable` (`:30`), `Issue::Webhookable` (`:64` → `app/models/concerns/issue/webhookable.rb:20`).

**Associations other records hang off (the teardown surface):**
- `belongs_to`: project, tracker, status, author, assigned_to, fixed_version, priority, category — `issue.rb:32-39`
- `has_many :journals as: :journalized, dependent: :destroy` — `issue.rb:41` (history)
- `has_many :time_entries, dependent: :destroy` — `issue.rb:42`
- `has_and_belongs_to_many :changesets` — `issue.rb:43`
- `has_many :relations_from / :relations_to, dependent: :delete_all` — `issue.rb:45-46`

**What a teammate must know:** the contract is `dependent:`-driven teardown mixing `:destroy` (journals, time_entries, attachments — run callbacks) vs `:delete_all` (relations, watchers, custom_values — skip callbacks); `safe_attributes` mass-assignment (`issue.rb:565`); the nested-set tree maintains lft/rgt + parent rollups; and reciprocal back-references live on `TimeEntry#issue` (`time_entry.rb:25`), `Journal#issue` (`journal.rb:27`), `IssueRelation#issue_from/issue_to` (`issue_relation.rb:38-39`).

## Step 3 — Lifecycle on create / update / destroy

**Trigger points:** `IssuesController#create`/`save_issue_with_child_records` (`issues_controller.rb:668`), `#update`, `#bulk_update` (`:349`), `#destroy` (`:431`); also `MailHandler#receive_issue` (`mail_handler.rb:190`) and `IssueImport#build_object` (`issue_import.rb:113`).

**Inline (in-transaction) effects, in callback order:**
- `before_save :close_duplicates, :update_done_ratio_from_issue_status, :force_updated_on_change, :update_closed_on` — `issue.rb:116`
- `after_save` project-change hook — `issue.rb:119`
- `after_save :reschedule_following_issues, :update_nested_set_attributes, :update_parent_attributes, :delete_selected_attachments, :create_journal` — `issue.rb:125` (parent/child rollups + history write; `create_journal` at `:2049` saves `current_journal`)
- `after_destroy :update_parent_attributes` — `issue.rb:124` (recalculates former parent; `recalculate_attributes_for` `:1863`)
- `destroy` override handles stale/already-deleted retry — `issue.rb:260`
- Dependent teardown fires here: journals/time_entries/attachments destroyed, relations/watchers/custom_values deleted.

**After-commit (out of transaction):**
- `after_create_commit :send_notification` (`issue.rb:132` → `send_notification` `:2099` → `Mailer.deliver_issue_add`) and `:add_auto_watcher` (`:133`/`:2090`)
- `after_commit :create_parent_issue_journal` — `issue.rb:134`/`:2055`

**Async:** notifications are `Mailer.deliver_*` → `deliver_later` (ActiveJob); update-notifications fire from the **Journal** side: `Journal after_create_commit :send_notification` (`journal.rb:66` → `journal.rb:374` → `Mailer.deliver_issue_edit` `:385`). Changeset linkage runs from `Changeset#fix_issue` (`changeset.rb:238`) on repo fetch.

## Step 4 — Every dependent of the Issue contract

**Request handling (controllers):**
- `ApplicationController#find_issue` `application_controller.rb:394` — loads + visibility-guards one issue
- `ApplicationController#find_issues` `:407` — loads + guards many
- `IssuesController#index/show/bulk_edit/bulk_update/destroy/build_new_issue_from_params/save_issue_with_child_records` `issues_controller.rb:45/95/262/349/431/592/668` — full CRUD + child-record save
- `AutoCompletesController#issues` `auto_completes_controller.rb:23` — issue autocomplete
- `ContextMenusController#issues` `context_menus_controller.rb:27` — bulk context menu
- `IssueRelationsController#find_issue` `issue_relations_controller.rb:102` — relation endpoints
- `PreviewsController#issue` `previews_controller.rb:24` — description preview
- `ProjectsController#show` `projects_controller.rb:170` — project overview counts
- `ReportsController#issue_report` / `#issue_report_details` `reports_controller.rb:25/45` — status/category reports
- `RepositoriesController#remove_related_issue` `repositories_controller.rb:264` — unlink changeset
- `SearchController#index` `search_controller.rb:24` — global search
- `TimelogController#find_optional_issue` `timelog_controller.rb:292` — time logging against issue
- `UsersController#show` `users_controller.rb:90` — user's assigned issues
- `VersionsController#index` / `#show` `versions_controller.rb:35/68` — roadmap

**Models & associations:**
- `Journal` `journal.rb:20` (`belongs_to :issue` `:27`), `Journal#visible_details` `:122`
- `TimeEntry` `time_entry.rb:20` (`belongs_to :issue` `:25`)
- `IssueRelation#safe_attributes=` `issue_relation.rb:86` (`belongs_to :issue_from/:issue_to` `:38-39`)
- `Changeset#fix_issue` `changeset.rb:238`, `#find_referenced_issue_by_id` `:212` — commit-keyword linkage
- `IssueCategory#destroy` `issue_category.rb:38` — reassign/nullify issues on category delete
- `IssueStatus.update_issue_done_ratios` `issue_status.rb:48`, `#handle_is_closed_change` `:97`, `#check_integrity` `:115`
- `IssueCustomField#visibility_by_project_condition` `issue_custom_field.rb:35`
- `IssueImport#build_object` `issue_import.rb:113` (+ `saved_objects` `:57`, `allowed_target_trackers` `:75`, `mappable_custom_fields` `:98`, `set_as_parent_callback` `:359`) — CSV import
- `TimeEntryImport#issue_project` `time_entry_import.rb:143`
- `Project#archive` `project.rb:418`, `#start_date` `:708`, `#due_date` `:718`, `#copy_issues` `:1141`, `#update_versions_from_hierarchy_change` `:1068`
- `Version#spent_hours` `version.rb:255`, `#update_issues_from_sharing_change` `:419`
- `Tracker#check_integrity` `tracker.rb:170`
- `User#notify_about?` `user.rb:864`, `#remove_references_before_destroy` `:990`
- `Group#remove_references_before_destroy` `group.rb:116`

**Reporting & queries:**
- `IssueQuery` `issue_query.rb:20` — entire query/filter engine (`base_scope` `:375`, `issues` `:406`, `issue_ids` `:457`, `available_columns` `:302`, plus ~25 `sql_for_*_field` filters `:505–:902`, `joins_for_order_statement` `:913`)
- `TimeEntryQuery` `time_entry_query.rb:20` — `sql_for_issue_*_field` `:196–:284`, `joins_for_order_statement` `:350`
- `Query#sql_for_field` `query.rb:1245` — base filter dispatch
- `Redmine::Helpers::TimeReport#load_available_criteria` `lib/redmine/helpers/time_report.rb:105`

## Step 1 — Orientation

## Step 5 — Identity & visibility guards

- **Request entry:** `ApplicationController#find_issue` `application_controller.rb:394` — loads by id then `raise Unauthorized unless @issue.visible?`; `#find_issues` `:407` requires `@issues.all?(&:visible?)`.
- **Core predicates:** `Issue.visible_condition(user, options)` `issue.rb:137` builds the SQL `WHERE` from role `issues_visibility` ('all'/'default'/'own'), is_private, author/assignee, and per-tracker view permission; `Issue#visible?(usr)` `issue.rb:172` is the in-memory equivalent via `allowed_to?(:view_issues, project)`. The `visible` scope `issue.rb:84` joins project + applies the condition.
- **Reporting/queries** funnel through the `visible` scope: `IssueQuery#base_scope` `issue_query.rb:375` / `#issues` `:406`.
- **Background email — reminders:** `Mailer.reminders` `mailer.rb:603` scopes to open + active-project + assigned, then re-filters per assignee `visible_issues = issues.select {|i| i.visible?(assignee)}` (`:638`).
- **Notifications:** `Issue#notified_users` `issue.rb:1148` ends with `notified.reject! {|user| !visible?(user)}` (`:1160`) so non-viewers never get mail; `User#notify_about?` `user.rb:864` adds per-user/project preference + visibility checks.
- **Autocomplete / context menu / cross-subsystem:** `AutoCompletesController#issues` `auto_completes_controller.rb:23` and `ContextMenusController#issues` `context_menus_controller.rb:27` both load via the visible scope / `find_issues`.
- **Parent-journal writes** re-guard: `create_parent_issue_journal` uses `Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id` `issue.rb:2071/2081`.
- **Gantt/Calendar** render only what the underlying `IssueQuery` (visible scope) returns.

## Step 6 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

Sense rates the symbol **high risk** (200 direct callers, 257 affected symbols, 66 files). Since the change is about *storing and tearing down attached data*, risk concentrates on teardown order, `dependent:` semantics, and callback timing.

**Highest risk (will break if teardown/callback contract changes):**
1. `IssuesController#destroy` `issues_controller.rb:431` — manually pre-computes descendants + handles `time_entries` nullify/reassign/destroy *before* `issue.destroy`; tightly coupled to the `dependent:` config.
2. `Issue` after_save/after_destroy callback chain `issue.rb:124-134` — reschedule, nested-set, parent rollups, journal creation, parent-journal; reorder/teardown changes cascade here.
3. `Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet` `issue_nested_set.rb:171/223` — parent/child lft/rgt integrity on destroy; corruption here is silent and hard to detect.
4. `acts_as_attachable` (`as: :container, dependent: :destroy`, `acts_as_attachable.rb:60`) + `acts_as_customizable` `before_destroy :store_attachment_custom_value_ids` `acts_as_customizable.rb:32` — attachment teardown interplay (custom-value file attachments).
5. `Journal` (`dependent: :destroy`, `journal.rb:20/27`) + `Journal#send_notification` `journal.rb:374` — history loss / spurious mail if journal teardown changes.
6. `Project#copy_issues` `project.rb:1141` and `Issue#copy_from` `issue.rb:306` — re-create the full attached-data set; mirror of teardown.

**Medium risk (derive data from attached records):**
7. `TimeEntry` (`belongs_to :issue`, `dependent: :destroy` `issue.rb:42`) + `Version#spent_hours` `version.rb:255` / `Issue.load_visible_total_spent_hours` `issue.rb:1251` — time rollups.
8. `IssueRelation` (`delete_all` `issue.rb:45-46`) + `reschedule_following_issues` `issue.rb:2000` — relation teardown affects scheduling.
9. `IssueQuery` / `TimeEntryQuery` SQL filters `issue_query.rb`, `time_entry_query.rb` — depend on columns/joins; schema-storage changes break them.
10. `Changeset` HABTM `issue.rb:43` + `Changeset#fix_issue` `changeset.rb:238` — join-row cleanup.
11. `Group#remove_references_before_destroy` `group.rb:116`, `User#remove_references_before_destroy` `user.rb:990` — assume nullable issue FKs.

**Lower risk (read/render only):** Gantt, calendar, My-page blocks, helpers (`format_object`, `show_detail`), search, autocomplete, migrations, Mantis/Trac rake tasks.

**Must re-verify after change:** destroy with time entries (all three `todo` modes), parent/child destroy + descendant cascade, journal history retention, attachment + custom-value file cleanup, relation/watcher cleanup, version spent-hours rollups, and edit/add notifications.
